mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in nonfarm payrolls signal broad economic strength and rising corporate revenues, leading to upward revisions in aggregate earnings forecasts and driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward Information Coefficient (IC) is consistently negative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate is near the zero lower bound, highly accommodative monetary policy reduces the equity risk premium and boosts asset valuations, leading to positive forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are not statistically significantly higher (p > 0.05) when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% compared to when it is higher.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously strong retail sales growth and declining housing starts indicate a late-cycle dynamic where consumer spending is robust but interest-rate sensitive sectors are weakening, signaling an impending economic slowdown and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: During an early-to-mid cycle recovery, accelerating industrial production reflects improving corporate operating conditions and robust demand, leading to higher earnings and positive short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.9% suggests mid-cycle, not recession
failure_condition: The correlation between INDPRO 3-month changes and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is negative during periods where GDP growth is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate over a short period signals a definitive start to a recessionary downturn, causing a sharp repricing of growth expectations and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month moving average rises > 0.5pp above its 12-month low
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 50% of instances following the trigger condition being met.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) increases the probability of future monetary policy tightening by the central bank, raising the discount rate for equities and leading to negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between month-over-month CPIAUCSL changes and subsequent 5-day S&P 500 returns is positive over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher year-over-year growth in real GDP reflects a fundamentally strong economy, which translates directly into higher aggregate corporate earnings and supports positive equity market performance over the medium term.
failure_condition: The correlation between year-over-year GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.1 over the full sample history.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a low unemployment rate and accelerating inflation creates maximum pressure on the central bank to tighten monetary policy. This 'overheating' signal leads to expectations of higher interest rates, compressing equity multiples and causing negative returns.
failure_condition: The average 20-day S&P 500 return is positive in the set of months where the trigger condition is met.
